title: chio-replay-report.v1
description: Stable JSON report shape emitted by `chio replay --json`. Mirrors the `ReplayReport` struct in crates/chio-cli/src/cli/replay/report.rs. The schema string `chio.replay.report/v1` is pinned by M04 Phase 4 task 5; the report is emitted on every `--json` run regardless of exit code so consumers can drive triage off a single byte stream.

schema.description: Stable schema identifier. Pinned to `chio.replay.report/v1`; downstream consumers MUST gate on byte-equal match before parsing the rest of the document.

log_path.description: Filesystem path the replay was driven against. Recorded verbatim from the `<log>` positional argument. Useful for triage when batch-replaying multiple corpora.

receipts_checked.description: Count of receipts the reader yielded and the verify/merkle/verdict pipelines processed. May be `0` only when an empty-corpus error short-circuited the run before any receipt was processed; in that case `exit_code` is non-zero and `first_divergence` carries a structural error.

computed_root.description: Lowercase-hex SHA-256 synthetic root produced by the streaming `MerkleAccumulator` over receipt canonical bytes in append order. Empty string when the run aborted before any receipt could be folded into the accumulator (matched only by the empty-corpus / pre-read failure case).

expected_root.description: Lowercase-hex root the run was asserted against (`--expect-root` flag). `null` when the user did not supply an expectation. When present and `computed_root != expected_root`, the divergence is reported as a `merkle_mismatch` kind and the exit code is the canonical merkle-divergence code (`0` is impossible on root mismatch).

kind.description: Divergence shape. The set of valid values mirrors the canonical exit-code registry in `.planning/trajectory/04-deterministic-replay.md` Phase 4 plus `merkle_mismatch` for the `--expect-root` mismatch case.

receipt_index.description: Zero-based index (in the order the reader yielded them) of the first receipt that exhibited the divergence. For pre-read structural errors (empty corpus, IO) this field is `0` and consumers MUST gate attribution on `kind`.

receipt_id.description: The `id` field of the offending receipt, when known. Absent for parse-error / pre-read shapes where the receipt could not be deserialized far enough to extract an id.

json_pointer.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointer to the offending field inside the receipt (e.g. `/decision/verdict` for verdict drift). Absent when the divergence is whole-receipt (signature mismatch) or pre-read.

byte_offset.description: Byte offset into the receipt log file where the offending receipt starts, when the reader can attribute it. Absent for directory-mode runs where per-file offsets do not compose into a single stream offset.

expected.description: Stored / expected value at `json_pointer`. For verdict drift this is the receipt's recorded decision label (`allow`, `deny`, ...). For merkle mismatch this is `expected_root`.

observed.description: Newly-derived / observed value at `json_pointer`. For verdict drift this is what the current build would produce. For merkle mismatch this is `computed_root`.

detail.description: Free-form human-readable failure note. Present for parse-error / verifier-error shapes that do not decompose into a structured `expected` / `observed` pair.

first_divergence.description: First divergence the pipeline observed, or `null` for a clean run. The pipeline order is: read -> signature verify -> verdict re-derive -> Merkle root compare. The first failing stage owns `first_divergence` and aborts subsequent stages.

exit_code.description: Process exit code the dispatch layer will use after writing the JSON report. Pinned by the canonical exit-code registry in M04 Phase 4: `0` clean, `10` verdict drift, `20` signature mismatch, `30` parse error, `40` schema mismatch, `50` redaction mismatch.
